frameworks.name: statistical regularities / stimulus expectation
frameworks.one_line: Predictability of repetitions implicitly cues target or distractor processing.

frameworks.name: ERP / oscillatory neural markers
frameworks.one_line: P1 amplitude and frontocentral theta index different expectation levels.

frameworks.name: multivariate stimulus decoding
frameworks.one_line: Decoding tests representation of distractor location and features.

core_question: Do immediate repetitions and higher-order expectations share neural mechanisms, and are target vs distractor expectations supported by the same processes?
key_assumptions: Statistical regularities in scenes can be implicitly learned
EEG/MEG signals can dissociate stimulus relevance from expectation order
manipulations: Predictability cued to target vs distractor location
Recording modality (EEG and MEG, separate studies)
key_findings: Reaction times decreased with expectation, driven by repetitions for both targets and distractors
P1 amplitude reduced for repeated distractors, increased for repeated targets
Higher-order expectations modulated frontocentral ERP amplitude and theta power, not P1
A single distractor repetition reduced decodability of its location and feature representation
interpretations: Target and distractor expectations share behavioral benefits but recruit distinct neural mechanisms
Immediate repetitions and higher-order expectations are dissociable in neural signatures
